_comment: Adapter registry for the arena runner. `cmd` is spawned from the repo root; the corpus is written to its stdin as JSONL and verdicts are read from stdout (see protocol.md). If a cmd can't be spawned (tool not installed, missing API key), the runner marks it unavailable and skips it. Competitor entries are roadmap: their adapter files are not committed yet — see README.md, and note which need an operator-provided API key (cost) versus which are OSS/local-runnable.

adapters.name: warden
adapters.notes: this repo, default posture (no LLM judge)

adapters.name: block-all (paranoid)
adapters.notes: sanity ceiling: 100% recall / 0% precision — proves recall alone is gameable

adapters.name: Meta LlamaFirewall (PromptGuard)
adapters.notes: PARTIAL-AXIS: an LLM-I/O guardrail; PromptGuard screens prompt-injection TEXT, not shell/tool-call actions. Fair only on the injection family (see README threat-model axes). Adapter is committed but UNAVAILABLE until `pip install llamafirewall` + a configured gated Meta HF model — the runner skips it until then.

adapters.name: Pipelock (scan API)
adapters.notes: PARTIAL-AXIS: Pipelock's primary layer is the egress wire (proxy DLP / SSRF / injection scanning); its documented per-call surface is the on-demand Scan API, which is what this adapter drives. Every sample gets every applicable kind (tool_call + dlp on the command string + prompt_injection on skill text), deny from any = block — composition can only raise its recall. Out-of-box posture: no mcp_tool_policy rules (operator-written rules would raise recall on command-semantics families — PRs welcome). Binary allow/deny. Needs a running daemon: `pipelock run --config arena/adapters/pipelock.arena.yaml` (the Arena workflow does this; pinned v3.0.0).

adapters.name: AEGIS (pre-execution check)
adapters.notes: SAME-AXIS: a pre-execution tool-call firewall — the first arena competitor with a genuine THREE-tier verdict. Scored via its documented `POST /api/v1/check` (an open route, no key) in blocking mode, so risky HIGH/CRITICAL calls return `pending` (escalate-to-human) → mapped to gated. Read the +gate column for AEGIS: its hard recall(block) is low BY DESIGN because it escalates rather than hard-blocks. Out-of-box `standard` policy template (shell/supply-chain/prompt-injection→block, file/db→MEDIUM, network→LOW); no operator DSL rules. Fresh agent_id per sample keeps its L2 behavioral-anomaly layer in the 'learning' phase (a stateless corpus can't build a per-agent baseline), so the scored verdict is the deterministic L1-policy + classifier + L3-DSL layer. AEGIS's /check screens the ACTION, not poisoned-skill TEXT, so injection-skill samples that carry no risky argument pass. Needs the gateway on :8080 (the Arena workflow builds + starts it from pinned commit 5f012d5 / v0.2.0).

adapters.name: mcp-firewall (inbound pipeline)
adapters.notes: SAME-AXIS: an MCP security gateway whose per-call surface is a tool-call firewall. Scored through its own inbound pipeline (kill-switch → rate-limit → injection → egress → policy → chain) in the documented `mcp-firewall init` starter posture, with the global rate-limit disabled (200-call window would deny the last ~45 of 245 on rate alone, not content). THREE-tier: deny→blocked, PROMPT→gated, allow→allowed. We read the RAW stage verdict because its SDK auto-approves prompts to allow on a non-interactive run, which would erase its human-approval tier. ESCALATE-BY-DEFAULT: the starter `defaultAction` is `prompt`, so anything not matched by injection/egress/a rule escalates to a human — expect HIGH +gate recall AND high friction (benign also escalates); read the two together. Injection detector screens prompt-injection TEXT, not shell semantics. Alpha (v0.1.0, no tagged release); pinned by commit 5f01d8b. AGPL-3.0: run as a black box via its SDK, not linked into warden. Needs `pip install git+https://github.com/ressl/mcp-firewall@5f01d8b` (the Arena workflow does this).

roadmap.name: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
roadmap.notes: OSS but rail checks are LLM-backed → needs a model endpoint (local or API). Non-deterministic; the determinism-contrast case. Partial-axis (dialog/rail checks, not tool-call classification).

roadmap.name: Claw Patrol (Deno)
roadmap.notes: NOT A LEADERBOARD ROW — different layer. Claw Patrol is a network-wire gateway: it parses protocol traffic (SQL verbs, K8s verb/resource, HTTP path) and gates via HCL rules + credential injection. It does not classify shell/tool-call strings, so this corpus cannot fairly score it. warden + Claw Patrol are complementary layers (the sprayberrylabs 'two layers' write-up), not competitors.

roadmap.name: Lakera Guard
roadmap.notes: cloud API — needs a paid LAKERA_API_KEY. Partial-axis (prompt-injection). Operator decision (key + budget) before wiring.

roadmap.name: mcp-firewall (ressl)
roadmap.notes: OSS MCP security gateway — policy enforcement on tools/call. Same-axis candidate; needs an adapter that drives its policy engine per-call.
